feed.title: UnixBSDShell
feed.subtitle: A Blog That Focuses On UNIX Systems, Such As FreeBSD OpenBSD NetBSD and Others

entry.title: How to Install mod_jk Module on Freebsd for Apache Tomcat

link.title: How to Install mod_jk Module on Freebsd for Apache Tomcat

entry.summary: Mod_jk is a complete replacement for the old mod_jser module that handles communication between Tomcat and HTTP servers using the Apache JServ protocol. The Apache mod_jserv module is the old bridge between Java and Apache.

entry.title: Configuring PHPMyAdmin and Nginx on OpenBSD 7.6

link.title: Configuring PHPMyAdmin and Nginx on OpenBSD 7.6

entry.summary: PHPMyAdmin is an open source software introduced on September 9, 1998, written in PHP.

entry.title: Setting up a crontab File on OpenBSD

link.title: Setting up a crontab File on OpenBSD

entry.summary: The active line in a crontab file is the setting of an environment variable or cron command. Setting an environment variable in a crontab file will create the environment in which any command in the crontab is run.

entry.title: SWAP Command In OpenBSD - SWAP File - swapctl swapon

link.title: SWAP Command In OpenBSD - SWAP File - swapctl swapon

entry.summary: Swap file is a file system that aims to create storage space on a hard disk or solid-state drive (SSD).

entry.title: FreeBSD MariaDB - How to Install and Configure

link.title: FreeBSD MariaDB - How to Install and Configure

entry.summary: MariaDB was first released in late 2009 to forever solidify the MySQL codebase as a free database management system.

entry.title: Running a PostgreSQL Database on OpenBSD

link.title: Running a PostgreSQL Database on OpenBSD

entry.summary: OpenBSD is a very robust, very secure and original Unix operating system, while PostgreSQL is a very stable, powerful, enterprise-grade, modern and full-featured relational database.

entry.title: FreeBSD14 and NGINX WITH mod_SSL PHP-FPM

link.title: FreeBSD14 and NGINX WITH mod_SSL PHP-FPM

entry.summary: Server blocks, known in Apache as virtual hosts, are the foundation of Nginx's ability to host multiple domains from a single server.

entry.title: Creating an Internet Router Gateway With FreeBSD and PF Firewall

link.title: Creating an Internet Router Gateway With FreeBSD and PF Firewall

entry.summary: Packet Filter (PF) is a firewall developed for OpenBSD and later ported to FreeBSD and NetBSD.

entry.title: FreeBSD Write img iso To Flashdisk For Bootable With dd Command

link.title: FreeBSD Write img iso To Flashdisk For Bootable With dd Command

entry.summary: In Unix commands, the dd utility is often referred to as a disk/data duplicator, it allows us to copy raw data from one source to another.

entry.title: How to Update the FreeBSD Ports System

link.title: How to Update the FreeBSD Ports System

entry.summary: The FreeBSD Ports Collection provides a simple and centralized way to install FreeBSD software.
